scope: Public-docs-based parity matrix. The 'rows' array covers Pipeline Builder, Workshop, Geospatial, Data Connection, Ontology Actions, and Functions at feature granularity. The 'module_summary' array rolls up all 25 foundry-*-1to1-checklist.md files so untracked modules (Compass, Slate, Marketplace, Vertex, Cipher, etc.) are not silently reported as 0%.

status_vocabulary.done: Feature is implemented, tested, documented, and verified through API or UI evidence.
status_vocabulary.partial: Some OpenFoundry surface exists, but behavior is incomplete or not wired end-to-end.
status_vocabulary.todo: No meaningful implementation found in the current repository inventory.
status_vocabulary.blocked: Implementation exists behind an unavailable dependency, missing runtime wiring, or an unmounted route.

module_summary_method.completion_rule: An item counts as 'done' only when both a handler file and a corresponding _test.go cover it. Items flagged needs-verification by the auditor are treated as not done in the headline percentage. Counts marked with leading ~ are within +/- 2 items because some checklist entries span multiple services.

verified_gaps: No Threads resource or thread document upload / export.
No Assist sidebar, custom content sources, or agent serving.
ReAct-style tool dispatch loop and step budgeting not wired end-to-end.
Evaluator types (built-in vs custom) and multi-target run orchestration not verified.
Branch-aware Logic editing and global-branching integration unimplemented.
Unit-test execution, test-case result storage, and experiments dataset integration sparse.
Contour is absent as a distinct analytical tool; no board / path / expression / parameter handlers.
Fusion spreadsheet, Code Workbook legacy, and ODBC / JDBC BI drivers entirely absent.
Export checkpoints, compute usage attribution, and refresh / freshness policies not implemented.
Environment topology and channel resource models entirely absent.
Promotion pipeline stages, per-stage approvals, and atomic multi-product promotion not wired.
Region-locked promotion, compliance-boundary enforcement, and canary / auto-rollback unimplemented.
Automate application overview, creation wizard, and detail page handlers absent.
Foundry Rules workflow, Rule Editor / Reviewer Workshop pages, and proposal lifecycle unimplemented.
Automation health monitoring, large-scale batching, and comprehensive audit trail not wired.
Stub service scaffolded in commit 7a34df8a; no algorithm registry, key resource, ciphertext envelope, or per-key access policy.
No encrypt / decrypt primitives or marking-aware decrypt.
No per-operation audit, AES-SIV deterministic mode, or cipher-aware join contract.
No central RID registry or RID validation; each service mints IDs locally.
No permission-aware search / catalog index; no search API or UI shell.
No trash / restore / hard-delete workflow; only basic project / org CRUD.
No function invocation dispatcher (CM.8) for function-mode jobs.
No pipeline-mode IO contract, run lifecycle, or data-connection adapters (CM.16-CM.20).
No scaling / autoscaling policy, health signals, or cold-start tracking (CM.21-CM.25).
Retention policy selectors and execution (DF.33-DF.35) only partially wired.
Data Lifetime lineage-aware policies (DF.36) absent.
Workflow Lineage handoff into actions / functions (DF.37) absent.
No release channels or automatic upgrade scheduling.
No resource-specific linters or supported-resource registry for Workshop / Pipeline / Ontology / Action packaging.
No input presets, linked-product graphs, or placeholder input generation.
Gateway route registration for /api/v1/functions/* and Helm/ArgoCD wiring are still pending.
Production isolation is still subprocess-based; deno/v8go, subinterpreters, or wasm isolation remains v1 work.
Repository blob fetch, ontology read/write client enforcement, staged-write accumulator, and action/Workshop adapters remain incomplete.
No fusion-spreadsheet-service with workbook / sheet / cell CRUD.
No sandboxed formula evaluator (SUM, VLOOKUP, IF, DATASET.LOOKUP, OBJECTS.LOOKUP).
No dataset sync layer with append / snapshot / upsert and conflict detection.

module_summary.owner: global-branch-service, code-repository-review-service (legacy route shape), workflow-automation-service (AIP Logic adapter)
verified_gaps: Gateway/frontend cutover from the legacy code-repository route shape to global-branch-service is still pending.
Branch detail / proposal detail / merge-check UI and taskbar integration remain absent.
Pipeline Builder / Ontology / Workshop / Functions / Object Views adapters, rebase, and atomic cross-application merge propagation are not implemented.
No standalone map-service wired in the gateway router table.
No tile service endpoint or OGC compliance layer.
No pluggable geocoder backend contract. (Workshop map widget is covered separately by the Workshop / Geospatial rows.)
No POST /media-sets/{rid}/upload handler; upload flow absent.
Preview / OCR / transcription access patterns unimplemented.
Data Connection media sync missing; no connector-management integration.
Model catalog has zero HTTP handlers (no model CRUD, versioning, adapters).
No Model Studio trainer registry, config wizard, or training execution.
No Modeling Objective CRUD, submission review, or release management.
All five production handler files (handlers.go, objects_bridge.go, links_traverse.go, validation.go, cedar_gate.go) lack test coverage.
No full-text, vector, spatial, or time-series indices.
No branching overlay writes, change-stream subscriptions, or snapshot / restore endpoints.
interfaces_shared.go and properties_links.go lack corresponding _test.go files.
No cost-based query optimizer; ontology-query-service has only basic search / graph paths.
No dedicated object-views-service; Object Views rendering integration path unverified.
No TypeScript codegen wired into the web UI or CLI.
No runtime client factory or fetch-based transport.
Marketplace OSDK packaging / distribution undefined.
No queue resource CRUD, worker classes, or admission API.
No unified job submission orchestration across builds / functions / notebooks.
No cost insights reporting or per-project / per-branch aggregates.
SG.10 permission-checking workbench absent (no access-graph visualization or explanation API).
SG.17 audit delivery / SIEM export / dataset backfill not wired.
SG.18 unified Admin Control Panel incomplete; no admin shell, change-requests, or admin-module gating.
No Slate page / route / widget schema or layout engine; handlers are generic CRUD stubs.
No Handlebars / variable / event / action framework; no query execution.
No Carbon workspace resource, module interfaces, or navigation framework.
P2 media / geospatial streaming synergy not yet wired.
Advanced stream replay (exactly-once vs at-least-once) partially stubbed.
Virtual media large-file materialization not integrated with media-sets-service.
ontology-exploratory-analysis-service is focused on geospatial / maps / writeback, not graph traversal.
No neighbor-expansion API with link-type / property filtering.
No graph layout engine, scenario planning, event timeline, or media-layer support.
P2 incremental preview / preview-skipping not fully wired.
Marketplace template distribution not surfaced for Pipeline / Workshop.
Media transform pipeline nodes (image / video / satellite) deferred.

rows.foundry_feature: Pipeline Builder: graph authoring and point-and-click pipeline editor
rows.notes: The React editor surface exists, including dataset import, join editing, lineage, logs, and deploy affordances. The matrix treats runtime execution, previews, outputs, and transform catalog as separate rows.

rows.foundry_feature: Pipeline Builder: persisted pipeline CRUD and authoring API
rows.notes: CRUD and route-shape parity are documented as closed when repositories are configured; product-level graph semantics still need the Pipeline IR contract from PB.1.

rows.foundry_feature: Pipeline Builder: branch, draft, proposal, publish, and history lifecycle
rows.notes: Draft DAG and published DAG are stored separately, versions record draft/proposal/published/restored snapshots, build execution reads the published DAG when available, and the editor can open proposals, publish drafts, list history, and restore prior versions.

rows.foundry_feature: Pipeline Builder: build lifecycle, jobs, logs, abort/retry, and run status
rows.notes: Runs now persist queued -> running -> succeeded/failed/cancelled, capture rich per-node node_results with lifecycle events, attempts, row counts, schema deltas, output resources, and log RIDs, and the run detail UI renders the historical explanation with abort/retry controls.

rows.foundry_feature: Pipeline Builder: transform execution runtime for SQL, Python, LLM, WASM, passthrough, and distributed compute
rows.notes: Runtime dispatch now uses existing OpenFoundry components: in-memory pipeline-expression table transforms for lightweight input/filter/select/output, Python sidecar dispatch for Python nodes, AI-service dispatch for LLM nodes, and Spark adapter dispatch for distributed nodes. WASM/remote remain future adapters.

rows.foundry_feature: Pipeline Builder: lightweight/faster pipeline type
rows.notes: pipeline_type=FASTER is persisted through authoring CRUD, LIGHTWEIGHT aliases normalize to FASTER, execution plans advertise preferred_runtime=lightweight_table, the create flow presents Lightweight / Faster, and the benchmark fixture exercises local table/expression execution without DuckDB/DataFusion.

rows.foundry_feature: Pipeline Builder: Python transform support
rows.notes: python_transform is in the catalog, sidecar-backed python nodes receive upstream rows as prepared_inputs/input_rows, typed output schemas feed validation, package allowlists and timeout clamps are enforced, stdout/stderr are captured, and result_rows become downstream table rows for output commits.

rows.foundry_feature: Pipeline Builder: Spark/Flink distributed pipeline type
rows.notes: pipeline_type=DISTRIBUTED is now a persisted authoring choice. The executor sends unchanged graph nodes through a DistributedTransformRunner; Spark/PySpark submit SparkApplication CRs through existing Kubernetes wiring, and Flink is a typed injected adapter boundary with an explicit config-gated error until a Flink runner is supplied.

rows.foundry_feature: Pipeline Builder: per-node preview and typed preview errors
rows.notes: Selected-node preview now POSTs the draft DAG or GETs a persisted pipeline, prunes execution to the target node's upstream chain, returns typed errors, and renders source/transform/join/union/output rows in the canvas preview panel.

rows.foundry_feature: Pipeline Builder: strict schema validation before build
rows.notes: Draft and persisted validation endpoints now return node/column/code/message diagnostics, and run start/retry paths fail before opening a build when missing columns, incompatible joins/unions, invalid casts, nullability, duplicate names, output primary keys, declared output schemas, or geospatial logical constraints are invalid.

rows.foundry_feature: Pipeline Builder: transform and expression catalog
rows.notes: pipeline_transform_catalog.v1 is served by API with typed form fields, default configs, output contracts, docs, and execution status for select/drop/rename, cast, filter, formula, normalize units, derive column, aggregate, sort, join, union, explode, JSON extract, CSV parse, output mapping, normalize column names, and the AI-service-backed llm_node. The stack editor consumes the catalog and falls back locally if unavailable.

rows.foundry_feature: Pipeline Builder: reusable functions and UDF nodes
rows.notes: Pipeline Builder exposes versioned registry functions in the transform catalog, including typed parameters and execution status, and the lightweight runtime can resolve and execute expression UDF nodes with function_auto_upgrade. Python UDF catalog entries are visible but remain function-bridge gated separately from Python transform nodes.

rows.foundry_feature: Pipeline Builder: join and union schema guidance
rows.notes: Draft and persisted guidance endpoints now suggest compatible join keys, flag join key type mismatches, report union missing/extra/incompatible columns, expose input/output schemas, and feed those diagnostics into the visual join/union editors.

rows.foundry_feature: Pipeline Builder: dataset outputs and build lineage
rows.notes: Output nodes now get stable target dataset IDs in the editor, successful executions POST inferred schema/preview/file metadata/lineage to dataset-versioning, and pipeline output metadata is marked committed only after the dataset commit succeeds.

rows.foundry_feature: Pipeline Builder: Ontology object and link outputs
rows.notes: Object outputs deploy backed object types/properties, and link outputs now deploy/update link types, validate source/target object outputs plus one-to-many/many-to-many join/FK columns, and materialize link rows into object-database.

rows.foundry_feature: Pipeline Builder: LLM node and AIP-assisted generation
rows.notes: llm nodes call the configured AI service, write outputs as ordinary downstream table columns, and preview through the selected upstream chain. AIP generation endpoints request normal PipelineNode JSON from the same provider, append it to the graph in the editor, and preview the generated node before returning.

rows.foundry_feature: Workshop: app/module/page/section/widget schema, CRUD, preview, publish, and public runtime load
rows.notes: WS.1/WS.2/WS.18/WS.19 are implemented for schema, frontend endpoint coverage, preview semantics, and publish/version/promote lifecycle: 2026-05-11.ws.1 normalizes pages/layouts/sections/overlays/widgets/configs/bindings/events/actions/variables/runtime metadata/public slug; draft preview/page CRUD/template/Slate endpoints are mounted; editor preview renders draft state through the same AppRenderer as public runtime; public runtime loads immutable app_versions.app_snapshot; and rollback/promote creates a new published version from a historical snapshot while preserving changelog notes, author, and timestamps. Remaining partial status is for broader Workshop builder parity beyond this lifecycle row.

rows.foundry_feature: Workshop: app permissions, public constraints, and audit
rows.notes: WS.20 is implemented: app routes require JWT-backed apps:view, apps:edit, or apps:publish access through roles or permission keys; denied edit/publish attempts return 403 and append app_audit_events; successful app mutations publish best-effort audit rows with actor, permission/status, request metadata, timestamps, and details; public runtime rejects archived apps and non-published snapshots; and Workshop-triggered Ontology actions already persist action_attempt records for single and batch execution.

rows.foundry_feature: Workshop: templates, widget catalog, Slate package import/export, and page CRUD backend endpoints
rows.notes: WS.2/WS.3 are implemented: the backend responds to the frontend's widget catalog, app templates, from-template, page CRUD, draft preview, Slate package import/export, and public embed calls. The widget catalog now comes from versioned backend data with config schemas, variables, events, permissions, and display metadata instead of local or Go hardcoded widget lists.

rows.foundry_feature: Workshop: visual page/section/widget editor
rows.notes: The editor and toolbar exist. Backend parity and variable/event behavior determine whether it reaches Foundry-style Workshop depth.

rows.foundry_feature: Workshop: object-aware widgets including Object Table, Filter List, Property List, Object Set Title, Chart XY, and Pie Chart
rows.notes: WS.4/WS.5/WS.8/WS.9/WS.10/WS.11/WS.14/WS.17 are implemented for shared variable/object-set execution, Object Table parity v1, Filter List parity v1, Property List parity v1, Chart XY parity v1, Object Set Title parity v1, and selected-object-set bulk actions. Object Table supports configured columns, typed sort toggles, row height/wrapping, active-object output, multi-select selected-object-set output, selection events, row actions, selected-row action buttons, and inline edits. Filter List emits reusable object-set filters. Property List reads object-set inputs, selected-object-set outputs, and active-object variables, displays the first object, supports selected property lists, null hiding, adjacent/below layouts, wrapping, and basic value formatting. Object Set Title supports count mode, single-object title mode, object type icon/color metadata, title overrides, hide-by-default empty sets, and configured empty placeholders. Chart XY reads object-set layers, renders bar/line/scatter series with property aggregation and legend/axes, and publishes selected categories as filter and selected-object-set outputs. Remaining parity work is mainly deeper Foundry-specific options on metric-style widgets and Pie Chart.

rows.foundry_feature: Workshop: Free-form Analysis widget
rows.notes: WS.21 is implemented for bounded runtime exploration: Free-form Analysis reads a configured input object set, lets users add filter/table/metric/bar/line/pie/text cards, searches and filters rows locally, saves analysis paths locally when enabled, renders charts through ECharts, and publishes the current filtered rows as an object_set_selection output variable for downstream widgets.

rows.foundry_feature: Workshop: variable engine and object set filter variables
rows.notes: WS.4 and ONT.4 are implemented: runtime variables now resolve through a central dependency graph with primitive, object-set, composable object-set filter, selected-object, aggregation, shape, URL, and runtime-parameter values. Object-set filters support defaults, runtime widget output, direct filter payloads, primitive/function-backed value references, and composition from other filter variables. Editor preview and published runtime share the same engine; object widgets and Map widget consume linked filter variables and selected object-set outputs through engine helpers rather than ad hoc per-widget state.

rows.foundry_feature: Workshop: events and navigational actions
rows.notes: WS.6 is implemented: trigger-matched events execute sequentially with handlers for set variable, set/clear runtime parameters, navigate, open URL, filter, seed prompt, refresh, apply action, export, and command fallback. Button Group clicks and Object Table row selection now dispatch event chains through the shared runtime.

rows.foundry_feature: Workshop: Button Group triggering Ontology Actions
rows.notes: WS.7/WS.16/WS.17/ONT.5 are implemented: Button Group and Object Table action buttons open a generic action form, resolve static/active-object/selected-object-set/variable defaults through the Workshop variable engine, honor visible/disabled/hidden parameter settings, render required and object-reference/list fields, show local/server validation issues, validate through the action API, execute create/edit/create-or-modify/delete/link/webhook/function action kinds through the generic single or batch endpoint, surface operation-specific success toasts, keep partial-failure bulk forms open with per-target errors, and refresh runtime widgets after completion.

rows.foundry_feature: Workshop: Map widget
rows.notes: WS.13 plus GEO.6/GEO.7/GEO.8/GEO.9/GEO.10/GEO.11/GEO.13 are implemented for Map widget v1: the editor exposes local object layer setup, overlay layer setup, base map/viewport settings, variable-backed visibility, selected-object and object-set outputs, drawn-shape outputs, shape search outputs, and map-template parameter/value mappings. Runtime MapLibre renders query-backed and object-set/object-type point/line/polygon layers, tooltips, legends, clusters, viewport-tile layers, saved/GeoJSON/MVT/raster overlays, and saved map templates. Selected map objects can drive Property List, Object Table, Object Set Title, and Button Group action defaults; drawn shapes can drive Object Table search results. Advanced geospatial joins/search remain future rows outside v1.

rows.foundry_feature: Workshop: Metric Card and function-backed display values
rows.notes: WS.12/WS.15 are implemented for Metric Cards and Function-backed variables: grouped numeric/string metrics resolve through the Workshop variable engine, render in card/tag/list styles, support static fallback values, number/integer/compact/percent/currency/unit formatting, prefix/suffix/empty-value handling, secondary values, and conditional formatting. function_output variables call Function packages with static and variable-bound parameters, compute stable cache keys, reuse successful results until dependencies or refresh change, expose results as primitive-compatible values, and let Metric Card display an effort estimator Function result.

rows.foundry_feature: Geospatial: public API routing for layers, query, clusters, geocode, tiles, templates, and routes
rows.notes: The normal exploratory-analysis binary now mounts /api/v1/geospatial/* with the production Postgres pool. The edge gateway routes /api/v1/geospatial to the consolidated service default on port 50131, and HTTP smoke covers overview, layers, query, clusters, tiles, and map templates.

rows.foundry_feature: Geospatial: map layer storage and layer CRUD
rows.notes: Layer models, migration, and handlers exist. Runtime availability depends on public route mounting.

rows.foundry_feature: Geospatial: feature query, within/intersects/nearest/buffer, and clusters
rows.notes: QueryFeatures and ClusterFeatures are implemented in the handler namespace; route_features/routing is explicitly not ported in that slice.

rows.foundry_feature: Geospatial: Map application UI and MapLibre rendering
rows.notes: The frontend has a dedicated geospatial page and shared MapLibre canvas. Workshop now covers object/overlay layer runtime toggles and saved map-template embedding. Full standalone Map app parity still needs richer saved-layer browsing and app-level drawing/search workflows.

rows.foundry_feature: Geospatial: logical types, CRS policy, GeoJSON/GPX parsing, Haversine, and geo joins in Pipeline Builder
rows.notes: GEO.2/GEO.3/GEO.4/GEO.5/GEO.12 are implemented with shared logical types, EPSG:4326 CRS policy, GeoJSON/bbox/H3 contracts, no-op EPSG:4326 reprojection, GPX track/route parsing, Pipeline Builder schema/CRS metadata checks, transform catalog gpx_parse plus geo_intersection_join/geo_distance_join/geo_nearest_neighbor_join, direct GPX upload parsing, unit-aware Haversine expression functions, nullable-coordinate distance propagation, a haversine_distance transform-stack block, Ontology property mappings, and lightweight _geo_join runtime support for intersection, distance-threshold, and KNN coffee recommendation joins with explicit scale limits.

rows.foundry_feature: Data Connection: source catalog, source CRUD, credentials, agents, and egress policies
rows.notes: The source/catalog/agent/credential/egress surfaces are mounted and tested. REST API source create/update now normalizes domain/base URL, auth, secret refs, runtime policy, permissions, and webhook defaults. Individual connector runtime depth still varies by adapter.

rows.foundry_feature: Data Connection: REST API source and virtual table preview/query
rows.notes: REST source configs now model outbound webhook needs and support test/query execution with configured auth/query defaults. Virtual table preview/query primitives exist; full webhook builder UX is separate.

rows.foundry_feature: Data Connection: outbound webhooks with input/output parameters
rows.notes: DC.2/DC.3/DC.5/DC.6 are implemented: webhook definitions model typed inputs, single/chained REST calls, templating, response extractors, typed outputs, timeout, concurrency, rate-limit, limits, and history settings. Invocation runs the model against legacy configs or rest_api source webhooks and enforces owner/invoke permissions, source invokable flags, method/egress policy, size limits, rate/concurrency limits, and sanitized diagnostics. Successful and failed invocations are persisted with user, timings, retention, input visibility policy, parsed outputs, and sanitized error details. TypeScript Functions can invoke configured webhooks through context.sdk.dataConnection.invokeWebhook. Persisted webhook authoring remains separate.

rows.foundry_feature: Data Connection: webhook integration with Action types
rows.notes: DC.4 is implemented: action execution supports Data Connection webhook writebacks before ontology edits, side-effect webhooks after successful edits, internal service JWT auth to connector-management, nested input mappings, typed output aliasing, explicit output mappings, and object-edit property mappings from webhook_output.*. Remaining parity work is persisted webhook history/external-function wrappers.

rows.foundry_feature: Data Connection: inbound listeners
rows.notes: DC.7 is implemented for HTTPS push listeners: source configs can define signed listeners, public receive routes verify HMAC/shared-secret auth, payload limits are enforced, auth headers are redacted, and JSON events are stored as queryable inbound_listener_events with event_log, dataset, or object destination metadata. Kafka/email/SQS/Pub/Sub-style listener families and direct downstream projection workers remain future parity work.

rows.foundry_feature: Trail Running demo: fixture pack and seed entrypoint
rows.notes: DEMO.1 is implemented with a privacy-safe synthetic fixture pack and deterministic seed output for raw Strava-like activities, normalized run activities, parsed trail rows, coffee-shop tables, an offline weather snapshot, and the published Workshop app fixture.

rows.foundry_feature: Trail Running demo: Strava RunActivity ingestion pipeline
rows.notes: DEMO.2 is implemented with a Python transform that filters Run/Trail Run activities, rejects private athlete/profile/device/email-style keys, normalizes distance/elevation/pace metrics, preserves HR, effort, terrain, surface, altitude, and weather profile fields, and declares Pipeline Builder dataset plus Ontology object outputs for RunActivity.

rows.foundry_feature: Trail Running demo: GPX Trail ingestion pipeline
rows.notes: DEMO.3 is implemented with a GPX transform and gpx_parse Pipeline IR that emit map-ready Trail rows with WGS84 GeoPoint strings, bbox, GeoJSON LineString route geometry, distance/elevation metrics, and dataset plus Ontology object outputs.

rows.foundry_feature: Trail Running demo: effort estimator function
rows.notes: DEMO.4 is implemented with estimateTrailEffort: top-N weighted KNN profile similarity over distance, gain, grade, altitude, terrain, surface, weather load, and HR profile; averaged pace/effort/heart-rate metrics; similar-run explanations; and confidence for selected-trail Workshop metric/detail panels.

rows.foundry_feature: Trail Running demo: coffee recommendation pipeline
rows.notes: DEMO.5 is implemented with a Haversine/nearest-neighbor coffee pipeline that emits top-3 cafes per trail, exact distance columns, map LineString geometry, CoffeeShop/TrailCoffeeRecommendation output contracts, and a TrailCoffeeLink table for selected-trail map/table flows.

rows.foundry_feature: Trail Running demo: weather webhook/action
rows.owner: tools/demo/trail-running, Data Connection, Ontology Actions, Workshop runtime
rows.notes: DEMO.6 is implemented with an Open-Meteo REST source/webhook, selected-trail action mappings, typed response extraction, sanitized webhook history settings, and a mocked HTTP action test that writes deterministic WeatherSnapshot payloads for Workshop Button Group flows.

rows.foundry_feature: Trail Running demo: Workshop app pages
rows.notes: DEMO.7 is implemented with a published run-fast Workshop app fixture: Trail Overview uses Filter List/Object Table/Chart XY/Metric Card/Button Group, Trail Map uses MapLibre object-set layers for trailheads, routes, cafes, and distance lines, and Trail Detail uses Object Set Title/Property List/Metric Card/Object Table. Playwright opens /apps/runtime/run-fast and navigates all pages against deterministic Trail/Coffee/Weather fixture objects.

rows.foundry_feature: Trail Running demo: custom GPX upload from Workshop
rows.notes: DEMO.8 is implemented with a Workshop Upload GPX page and media_uploader GPX mode that sends multipart files to the GPX parser endpoint, normalizes parser output into Trail properties, invokes estimateTrailEffort, creates a TrailEffortEstimate, and refreshes object-set backed tables in the runtime.

rows.foundry_feature: Trail Running demo: smarter similarity scoring
rows.notes: DEMO.9 is implemented with weighted_knn_trail_profile_v2, a deterministic weighted vector score over distance, elevation gain, grade, altitude, terrain, surface, weather load, and HR profile. Estimates expose feature_weights, target_profile, target_similarity_vector, feature_scores, weighted_feature_scores, similarity_vector_delta, and match_explanation so Workshop can explain why runs were selected.

rows.foundry_feature: Quality gates: route smoke tests for gateway and service routers
rows.owner: edge-gateway-service, pipeline-build-service, ontology-exploratory-analysis-service, application-composition-service, ontology-actions-service, connector-management-service
rows.notes: QA.1 is implemented with gateway upstream smoke tests plus service-level chi.Walk route contracts for Pipeline Builder, Geospatial, Workshop/App Builder, Ontology Actions, and Data Connection. The gateway now routes /api/v1/webhooks/* and /api/v1/listeners/* to connector-management so action/webhook and inbound listener calls are not orphaned.

rows.foundry_feature: Quality gates: fixture-driven Trail Running pipeline execution
rows.notes: QA.2 is implemented with a single fixture-driven acceptance test that executes Strava JSON ingestion, GPX trail ingestion, effort estimation, coffee recommendations from JSON and CSV inputs, weather webhook/action writeback, and seed.py bundle generation against deterministic golden outputs.

rows.foundry_feature: Quality gates: Workshop runtime Playwright critical path
rows.notes: QA.3 is implemented with a fixture-backed published Workshop runtime test that covers Object Table selection, Filter List recompute, Button Group weather action validate/execute, Chart XY canvas rendering, MapLibre trail/coffee layers, Trail Detail widgets, public runtime load, attached screenshots, and Playwright trace collection.

rows.foundry_feature: Ontology Actions: action type CRUD, schema, rules, validation, and submission criteria
rows.notes: Action type CRUD/validation primitives exist and accept a broad operation-kind enum. Some rule effects remain gated or service-dependent.

rows.foundry_feature: Ontology Actions: apply/execute actions for object edits, links, webhooks, and functions
rows.notes: ONT.5 is implemented: the action runtime accepts the expanded operation-kind contract for create, update/modify, create-or-modify, delete, create/delete link, webhook invocation, function invocation, interface actions, side-effect webhooks, function response edits, and server-side validation. Workshop continues to use the same generic validate/execute/execute-batch endpoints; deeper action-log object materialization and revert UX remain separate rows.

rows.foundry_feature: Ontology Actions: action log, audit publication, metrics, and revert
rows.notes: ONT.6 is implemented: action submissions now materialize as queryable synthetic ActionLogRecord ontology objects with operation id, actor, target ids, parameters, validation, edits/result, status, failure type, duration, and timestamp for single, batch, and inline-edit paths. The broader row remains partial until user-facing log browsing/revert UX is completed.

rows.foundry_feature: Functions: function package CRUD, templates, authoring metadata, and simulation
rows.notes: Function handlers are mounted from ontology-actions-service and kernel tests cover function package behavior. Generated SDK-style Functions on Objects parity is still lighter than Foundry.

rows.foundry_feature: Functions: inline Python and TypeScript runtime execution
rows.notes: Inline runtime parsing/validation exists; TypeScript runtime includes a Data Connection external Functions wrapper for configured webhook calls. Python execution depends on sidecar configuration.

rows.foundry_feature: Functions: function-backed actions
rows.notes: Action definitions now resolve package-backed or inline Function rules before create/update, reject mixed Function plus Ontology rules, and execute package-backed functions through the normal action runtime while applying returned object/link/delete edits. WS.16 verifies Workshop can submit a function-backed action form.

rows.foundry_feature: Functions: object sets, filtering, search around, aggregations, and KNN-style retrieval
rows.notes: Workshop object-set consumption now uses one declarative query contract for object type starts, chained sets, selected objects, saved sets, filters, sort, count, aggregations, geospatial search-around, link-type search-around traversal, and KNN vector retrieval over numeric vector/embedding properties. Map can render returned linked_edges as relationship layers between visible object endpoints. Broader generated Functions SDK-style object-set authoring remains partial.
